education.thesis: Deep learning-based tumour segmentation in MRI scans using U-Net variants

publications.title: Attention-gated U-Net for brain tumour segmentation in multi-modal MRI

projects.title: Federated learning for privacy-preserving cancer detection
projects.description: Built a federated training pipeline across 3 simulated hospital nodes using PySyft; achieved within 2% of centralised baseline on TCGA dataset.

projects.title: Survival prediction from histopathology slides
projects.description: Weakly supervised multiple-instance learning on CAMELYON16; ROC-AUC 0.91.

research_interests: explainable AI in clinical decision support
intro_call_summary: Priya is very strong technically — MICCAI publication at undergrad is unusual. She is particularly excited about computational pathology and federated learning for privacy in multi-site clinical studies. She mentioned Prof. Gustavo Carneiro's work on weakly supervised learning in pathology as the type of research she wants to do. She is open to both CS and BME departments as long as the research is applied to healthcare. She has a strong GPA and a published paper so she can aim for competitive labs. Not interested in purely theoretical ML — wants to see real clinical translation. Budget: fully funded positions only.
raw_resume_text:
Priya Sharma | IIT Bombay B.Tech Biomedical Engineering 2024 | CGPA 9.1/10
Publications: MICCAI Workshop 2023 (first author) — attention-gated U-Net for brain tumour segmentation
Projects: Federated learning for cancer detection (PySyft), survival prediction from WSI (CAMELYON16)
Skills: Python, PyTorch, MONAI, Docker, R, MATLAB
Awards: Institute Silver Medal, IIT Bombay 2024
